data.narrative:
Tonbo Imaging, a Series D defense and security technology company, has raised $66.5M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Saronic, Chaos Industries, Epirus, Onebrief, and Anduril Industries, all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $66M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $2.9B, with observed range spanning $1.1B (Onebrief) to $9.2B (Saronic). The dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in stage maturity and commercial traction within Series D defense tech.

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated range of $85M (low), $1.5B (median), and $3.4B (high). The wide spread reflects the core tension between Tonbo's capital efficiency relative to early-stage metrics and peer valuations driven by late-stage funding rounds and differentiated product positioning. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and margin trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, customer concentration in Indian defense procurement, and technology differentiation versus larger incumbents. The median of $1.5B represents a reasonable centering estimate pending visibility into recent operational milestones.
